summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a pivotal 2/21/71 Port Chester show with standout Ripple, Easy Wind and Truckin’ performances
review: Fans rave about this recording as one of the best from the early ’70 run – a historic first without Mickey yet still driving, with Jerry’s stage antics, Phil’s cord‑spinning and Pigpen saving a flub in Easy Wind. The soundboard source is generally praised as clear and well‑balanced, though a few listeners note oddities on Ripple, but overall the audio holds up nicely.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈80 %) are 4‑5 stars and highlight exceptional musical moments; recording quality is described as good to excellent, yielding a strong combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers say the sound is clear and well‑balanced. A couple of comments note Ripple sounds odd or “ruined” by the recording, but overall the mix is praised as solid with no major drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert is described as an enthusiastic, focused performance marking a key transition in the band’s evolution – first show without Mickey yet still tight, with strong first‑set energy and memorable jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Several reviewers note Jerry bobbing on stage and his usual charismatic presence.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil is mentioned spinning his cord during the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pig (Ron "Pigpen" McKernan) saved a flub in Easy Wind with his lyrics, earning praise.
band_member_comments.Drums: First show without Mickey; drums still on stage (Bill Kreutzmann) and keep solid time.
